rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Motion For Summary Judgment (Added For The Entry Of Tentative Ruling Purposes Only)
rulings.Rulings: [This tentative ruling is posted after 3:00 p.m.] [Part 2 of 2] Petitioner's request that the motion be denied or hearing on the motion be continued due to pending discovery is denied. Petitioner did not make the required showing under CCP section 437c, subd. (h). And the record belies Petitioner's unsupported request under section 437c(h). These Respondents filed a substantially identical summary judgment motion based on substantially identical evidence in November 2019 and Petitioner requested denial or a continuance of that motion under section 437c(h). There is no evidence that Petitioner developed the record in any relevant way in the intervening 11 months. There is no evidence that any party unreasonably prevented discovery. In fact, over a year ago, these Respondents responded to Petitioner's only discovery demand addressed to them and Petitioner pursued no further discovery. Respondents' alternative motion for summary adjudication is DENIED AS MOOT. Respondents Linqvist and Ingeborn are ordered to lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on October 8, 2020. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. [Part 2 of 2]

[This tentative ruling is posted after 3:00 pm] [Part 1 of 2] This tentative is issued by Hon. Joseph M. Quinn The Court grants Respondents' Request for Judicial Notice. The Court grants Petitioner's Request for Judicial Notice. As to both requests, notice is limited to the relevant facts properly subject to notice given the materials presented and does not, for example, extend to the truth of assertions in pleadings or transcripts. (See Richtek USA, Inc. v. UPI Semiconductor Corp. (21015) 242 Cal.App.4th 651, 659-660.) The court SUSTAINS Respondents' evidentiary objections ## 1-9; 12-23. Ruling on objections ## 10 and 11 is not necessary for disposition of this motion. The court notes that a verified petition may be admitted as evidence in an uncontested proceeding, but not in a contested proceeding over the contestants' objection. (See Prob. C., section 1022.) Respondents Linqvist and Ingeborn's motion for summary judgment on the theories of liability alleged against them in the Second Amended Petition ("SAP") is GRANTED. (Code Civ. Proc., section 437c, subd. (f)(1).) In the SAP, Petitioner alleges that decedent died having a claim to real property known as 64 Potomac Street and the property or some interest therein is claimed by others, including Respondents, or decedent died having a claim to 64 Potomac Street, title to or possession is claimed to belong to others, including Respondents, and requests that the court order damages (e.g., return of "secret profits") and that title to the real property be returned to the estate/trust. (See Prob. C., section 850, subds. (a)(2)(C)&(D).) In their moving papers, Respondents make a prima facie showing that they are bona fide purchasers for value. They (1) paid valuable consideration for their interest in the real property (2) in good faith and (3) recorded their interest via Grant Deed on May 17, 2018. (Reiner v. Danial (1989) 211 Cal.App.3d 682, 689; see Resps' SSUMF ## 1-3, 6, 9-11.) Although the April 30, 2018 lis pendens was recorded at the time of the sale to Respondents, Respondents have made a prima facia showing under CCP sections 405.60 and 405.61 that they were not parties to this litigation when Petitioner recorded the lis pendens, they took title before the lis pendens was expunged, the lis pendens was ordered expunged on May 23, 2019, and that order was recorded with the SF Assessors Office the following day. (See Resps' SSUMF ## 10-11.) Petitioner has not set forth admissible evidence showing a triable issue of material fact exists as to Respondents' status as bona fide purchasers or that they are entitled to the protections of sections 405.60 and 405.61. (See Code Civ. Proc., section 473c, subd. (b).) Petitioner fails to cite any evidence-let alone evidence raising a dispute of material fact-in his opposition. Given the court's ruling on Respondents' evidentiary objections, Petitioner's response to the separate statement of undisputed material facts is entirely unsupported, meaning that Respondents' separate statement is entirely unrebutted. The court has, nevertheless, reviewed the admissible evidence proffered in Petitioner's Request for Judicial Notice and finds no support for a disputed material fact regarding Respondents Lindqvist and Ingeborn's status as bona fide purchasers of the subject property. [Part 1 of 2]
